---
title: "Python binaries"
description: "Short and sweet"
author: "Lisa"
date: "2026-03-06"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
editor: source
toc: true
image: "img/"
draft: false
freeze: true
filters:
   - lightbox
lightbox: auto
categories:
  - Posit
---

There are many  options for installing and managing your python versions. 

My current favorite way to do this is with [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/guides/install-python/). You can run commands that will install and manage your python binaries for you: 

```bash
# Install a psecific version of python 
uv python install 3.12

# Check aailable and installed python versions 
uv python list
```

uv will use the Python requirements defined in requires-python in the pyproject.toml file like this: 


```bash
[project]
name = "python-shiny-app-demo-cicd-github-actions"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Add your description here"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.12.11"
dependencies = [
    "altair>=5.4.1",
    "anywidget>=0.9.13",
    "pandas>=2.2.3",
    "shiny>=1.2.1",
    "shinywidgets>=0.3.4",
]
```

Uv/Astral is also building binaries that can be used to install Python in air-gappped or validated environments, but finding the right pages is a bit tricky: 

- Location for releases: <https://github.com/astral-sh/python-build-standalone/releases>

- There is documentation on the binaries here: <https://gregoryszorc.com/docs/python-build-standalone/main/running.html#obtaining-distributions>

- For an example I've found this repo that Michael made very useful: <https://github.com/michaelmayer2/py-install/>

- It would also be possible to use the now un-maintained Post method to build Python RPMs: <https://github.com/rstudio/python-builds>
